about.title: About İTTA
about.subtitle: A digital mapping and analysis platform for the Islamic historiographical tradition
about.what_text: İTTA (Atlas of Islamic Historiography) is a research platform that digitally maps, visualizes, and analyzes the historiographical tradition of Islamic civilization. The project presents scholars active across a vast geography from the 7th to the 20th century, their works, and the relationships between them through interactive maps, network graphs, and chronological visualizations.
about.method_text: Data is compiled from comprehensive regional studies in Macit Bey's MHTT (Historiography in Muslim Regions) project and enriched with data from the TDV Encyclopedia of Islam (DIA). Teacher-student relationships, contemporaneity ties, and geographic distributions are analyzed using digital methods.
about.source_mhtt: Historiography in Muslim Regions — historiographical research covering Iran, Egypt, the Indian Subcontinent, and other regions.
about.source_dia: TDV Encyclopedia of Islam — 8,500+ biographical entries, teacher-student relations, geographic data, and importance scores.
about.team_macit_skills: Domain expertise · Source validation · Coordination with 14 regional authors · İLEM board · İTTA vision
about.team_macit_affil: Kocaeli University, Faculty of Theology, Islamic History Dept.
about.team_ali_skills: NLP pipeline · Knowledge Graph · Web platform · ML modeling · Data architecture
about.team_ali_affil: Selçuk University, Faculty of Technology, Computer Engineering
about.team_huseyin_skills: Data validation · Annotation coordination · Region-based quality control
about.team_huseyin_affil: Selçuk University, Faculty of Islamic Sciences, Islamic History Dept.
about.team_coord_skills: Project coordination · Workshop organization · Institutional partnership management
about.team_assistants: Assistants: Elif Özdoğan Ulu, Haşem Gözgü · 14 Regional Directors + 60 Researchers
